seo_title: Current Time in Hong Kong — HKT (UTC+8)
seo_description: Current local time in Hong Kong. HKT (UTC+8) year-round, no daylight saving time. Live clock, HKEX trading hours, and time difference to New York, London, Sydney.

faq.question: What time is it in Hong Kong right now?
faq.answer: Hong Kong uses Hong Kong Time (HKT, UTC+8) year-round. The IANA timezone identifier is Asia/Hong_Kong. Hong Kong does not observe Daylight Saving Time. The IANA identifier is Asia/Hong_Kong. Hong Kong operates on Hong Kong Time (HKT/UTC+8) year-round. Hong Kong does not observe daylight saving time, so its UTC+8 offset is constant throughout the year. This makes Hong Kong 8 hours ahead of London (GMT), 13 hours ahead of New York (EST), and in the same time zone as Beijing, Singapore, and Perth.

faq.question: What time zone is Hong Kong in?
faq.answer: Hong Kong is in the Hong Kong Time (HKT) zone, which is UTC+8 year-round. HKT is identical to China Standard Time (CST), Singapore Time (SGT), and Australian Western Standard Time (AWST). Despite being a Special Administrative Region of China, Hong Kong uses the same UTC+8 offset as the mainland.

faq.question: Does Hong Kong observe daylight saving time?
faq.answer: No. Hong Kong abolished daylight saving time in 1979 and has maintained a constant UTC+8 offset ever since. This provides stable, predictable scheduling for international business — the time difference between Hong Kong and other cities never changes throughout the year, making it a reliable anchor for Asia-Pacific business coordination.

faq.question: What is the time difference between Hong Kong and New York?
faq.answer: Hong Kong is 13 hours ahead of New York during Eastern Standard Time (November–March) and 12 hours ahead during Eastern Daylight Time (March–November). When it is 9:00 AM in New York (EST), it is 10:00 PM in Hong Kong. Business overlap between Hong Kong and New York is minimal — early morning New York (6:00–9:00 AM EST) aligns with Hong Kong's late evening (7:00–10:00 PM HKT).

faq.question: What is the time difference between Hong Kong and London?
faq.answer: Hong Kong is 8 hours ahead of London (GMT) in winter and 7 hours ahead during British Summer Time (BST). When London opens at 9:00 AM GMT, it is 5:00 PM in Hong Kong — leaving a 30-minute to 1-hour overlap before Hong Kong's close of business. This narrow window makes early morning London calls (8:00–9:00 AM GMT) the most effective for reaching Hong Kong colleagues.

faq.question: What time does the Hong Kong Stock Exchange open?
faq.answer: The Hong Kong Stock Exchange (HKEX) opens at 9:30 AM HKT with a morning session running to 12:00 PM, then an afternoon session from 1:00 PM to 4:00 PM HKT. This translates to 1:30 AM–8:00 AM EST for New York traders. HKEX is the world's fifth-largest stock exchange, serving as the primary gateway for international investment into mainland Chinese companies.

faq.question: What is the best time to call Hong Kong from the US?
faq.answer: From New York, the best time to call Hong Kong is early morning: 7:00 AM–9:00 AM EST corresponds to 8:00 PM–10:00 PM HKT — late evening Hong Kong, often still workable in the financial sector. From Los Angeles, call between 4:00 AM and 7:00 AM PST for the same window. Scheduling the night before with a morning Hong Kong follow-up is a common strategy.

faq.question: Is Hong Kong time the same as China time?
faq.answer: Yes. Hong Kong Time (HKT/UTC+8) is identical to China Standard Time (CST/UTC+8) used across mainland China. Despite China spanning multiple geographic time zones, the entire country uses a single UTC+8 offset. Hong Kong, as a Special Administrative Region, independently maintains the same UTC+8 standard — a coincidence of history rather than a policy requirement.
